item.title: Four-star General Donahue who resigned after pressure tied to Pete Hegseth’s leadership. This is the respect he was shown as he left his home and his post. How can we trust our military leadership now? Time to put the Constitution first.

item.title: Random but funny
item.description:
Welcome to this dump of random memes. Sometimes I make them themed, sometimes I don't. If you enjoy laughing without talking politics, getting away from the horrors of the world, or escaping reality, you’ll love following this page!
Maybe she's born with it
This is Beautiful
Mine is Betty TripStrawberryJam
It looks glued on and then colored with a permanent marker
Man she got a glow up
Feel the rain on your skin
At least try to be kind
Heck yeah! Video games and fairies
Couldn't they just have used a chunky watch?
All the types of data
I haven't watched twin peaks, so I'll add aquarium breaking and sharks eat me
Wow. That is creepy
I hope it went into your stomach and not some other hole
I live for grilled hot dogs
Forgot the chips
Yes, we are
Happy anniversary lol
Are you blind?
Love a nerd
For reals
I think puns are funny. Hubby does not. I'm unappreciated in my time.
Happens to me a lot.
Hisssss
Both great movies too
Perfect
Just for an hour to hold because babies are so beautiful and I miss mine
*granted
Which are you?
It's SUCH a sacrifice
Oops
After you turn thirty, you always feel like this
Here's another
Hubby once told me to calm my tits. Never ever heard that before so I laughed so hard.
Goose goose
Autumn lasts sooooo long
We all want this love
I love this idea
So cute!
It really does look like that
Bet they regretted that headline cutoff
Hmm candy with cold lettuce and ham?
Still somehow addicting
Did I just have a stroke?
I agree. So school House Rock
Definitely a fart filled meal
Good. It's ugly anyway.
My aunt had a bunch when I was a kid
Hubby hates dolls. Would be hilarious to get him one.
Or perhaps have a special needs child that needs the extra security?
item.dc:creator: GigglySprinkleCupcake

item.title: 159th year since its establishment, handmade grape candy like a gem presented to the Emperor
item.dc:creator: Bombaso

item.title: GovBrief Today #521
item.description: Three Supreme Court justices just voted to end birthright citizenship, and the Speaker of the House wants to finish the job. The Court ruled 6 to 3 that the 14th Amendment protects citizenship for every child born on American soil. Donald Trump demanded Congress end it anyway, promising his "Complete and Total Support." By Sunday, Mike Johnson was on Fox News vowing to immediately advance a bill. Johnson is a constitutional lawyer. The guarantee he wants to legislate around has stood since 1868, and nothing about it changed last week. What changed is that Trump lost in court and ordered a workaround, and the Speaker went on national television to volunteer. I've emailed 8,104 stories over 521 straight nights, free and without ads. The algorithm can't show them all to you. Add your email address at govbrief.today and I will. Subscribe (It's free!): https://gbounacos.substack.com/ Today's full brief: https://go.govbrief.today/260704-full [1] https://go.govbrief.today/iran-war-july5 [2] https://go.govbrief.today/smithsonian-trust [3] https://go.govbrief.today/memphis-guard-shooting [4] https://go.govbrief.today/isaacman-f5s [5] https://go.govbrief.today/johnson-birthright [6] https://go.govbrief.today/4th-crowd-size [7] https://go.govbrief.today/trump-racist-slop [8] https://go.govbrief.today/red-card [9] https://go.govbrief.today/trump-overruled [10] https://go.govbrief.today/pool-repairs [11] https://go.govbrief.today/shelter-reading Subscribe (It's free!): https://gbounacos.substack.com/
item.dc:creator: GeorgeBounacos

item.title: This is Nnani Adaobi Maryanne. Russia killed her.
item.description: Ulraine's Kharkiv National Medical University has announced the death of 23-year-old Nigerian student Nnani Adaobi Marian, who succumbed to injuries sustained during a Russian attack on Ukraine. After being critically wounded, Marian was treated first in Kharkiv and later transferred to Germany, where doctors fought to save her life. Despite their efforts, she passed away on July 5. Marian began studying medicine at the university in 2020 and was remembered as a dedicated, talented and compassionate student. She participated in international academic programs, including internships at the University of Cambridge in 2024 and Turkey's Beruni University in 2025, where she expanded her medical knowledge and research experience. Her death is another reminder that Russia's war continues to claim the lives of civilians from around the world who came to Ukraine to study, work and build their futures. Kharkiv National Medical University extended its condolences to her family, friends and classmates, honoring her memory as an integral part of the university community. https://www.lettstv.com/article/russian-airstrike-kills-nigerian-medical-graduate-in-ukraine-days-before-graduation
item.dc:creator: vladrichdemaclant

item.title: How It Starts
item.description: Donold tRump Senior isn't 1943 Hitler (yet), but he is 1933 Hitler with the same attacks on free speech, the news media, education, minorities, other branches of government, bureaucrats, senior military leaders, science, the poor, foreigners, other religions, and the justice system.

Like what we do and how we do it? Leave a tip. Or don't. It's totally cool either way. Stay True & Stay Tuned - Meta Pathos
item.dc:creator: MetaPathos

item.title: You collect money? I collect cat videos.
item.description: I don't know if any of these are AI, I just like cats.
item.dc:creator: rreedican

item.description: For four years after the oil crisis, no one could prove where the heat was going. The original Princeton House Doctor project, led by researcher Gautam Dutt, tried every trick to find leaks: smoke pencils, infrared cameras, hand-held wind meters at suspected gaps. Nothing produced repeatable numbers. The breakthrough came when the team realized they needed to pressurize the entire house at once, then measure the airflow required to hold that pressure. The first blower door was crude. A window fan, a plywood panel cut to fit a doorway, and a U-tube manometer made of clear plastic tubing. It cost under $100 in 1977 dollars to build. Modern calibrated units cost about $3,500 and measure airflow accurate to within 3 percent. The technology is the same as it was in 1977. The fan depressurizes the house to 50 pascals, and the airflow required to hold that pressure equals the leakage rate. A home that was a black box for thousands of years became a measurable system in one summer in New Jersey....
item.dc:creator: TheRainbowegoSweet007

26 Man Tax: Adrien Brody
"What a strange illusion it is to suppose that beauty is goodness." - Leo Tolstoy So, I made a discord server. It's a relaxed environment where you can take/donate memes to the cause, play trivia twice a week, chat about lots of topics or about nothing at all. We also have Jack Box game night and are setting up movie night! Very laid back/relaxed vibe. Come hang with us. https://discord.gg/AWFV7FT3RQ I'm also on the imgcat server for updates & fun: https://discord.gg/TpjD37jgJ9 https://ko-fi.com/mememuseumcurator Unable to work, zero income. Feel free to leave me a tip. Those that have, y'all are the bee's knees. You've honestly been my lifeline. For the couple people that have said I'm "scamming" people, I only get SNAP, so this money goes to things like TP, shampoo, laundry detergent, etc.
item.dc:creator: MemeMuseumCurator
